question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger or influence the prune() deletion boundary by submitting ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions with crafted consensusTimestamp values that collide with the retention cutoff, causing legitimate in-flight topic messages to be permanently deleted before subscribers can receive them, effectively suppressing gossip of those transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the JPQL query 'delete from TopicMessage where consensusTimestamp <= ?1' by flooding the topic_message table with high-volume low-timestamp messages, causing the retention job to prune a disproportionately large batch that includes legitimate transactions, preventing their gossip to downstream subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial-of-gossip by submitting transactions with consensusTimestamp values just below the retention boundary, such that prune() deletes them before the gRPC subscriber's historical retrieval phase completes, resulting in a permanent gap in the message stream that is never recovered?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicMessage.isNew() always returns true (skipping Hibernate SELECT-before-INSERT) to submit a duplicate consensusTimestamp via a crafted transaction, causing a database unique constraint violation that rolls back the entire batch insert and silently drops multiple legitimate topic messages from being persisted and gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the inclusive boundary condition 'consensusTimestamp <= ?1' in prune() by timing a high-frequency message submission burst so that messages with the exact cutoff timestamp are deleted, preventing those specific transactions from ever being gossiped to active subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user prevent gossip of a specific transaction by submitting a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a sequenceNumber that creates an artificial gap, causing the missingMessages() recovery logic to issue a bounded retrieval query that excludes the target transaction due to an off-by-one in the limit calculation (numMissingMessages = current.getSequenceNumber() - last.getSequenceNumber() - 1)?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RetentionJob's batch-based prune loop to cause a race condition where prune() is called concurrently with a subscriber's historical retrieval, deleting topic messages mid-stream and causing the subscriber to receive an incomplete sequence that silently omits gossiped transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user prevent gossip of multiple transactions by exploiting the absence of a lower-bound check in prune()'s JPQL query, where a misconfigured or manipulated consensusTimestamp parameter of Long.MAX_VALUE would delete the entire topic_message table, suppressing all pending gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMessage entity's nullable chunkNum and chunkTotal fields to submit a fragmented message with inconsistent chunk metadata, causing the importer to persist a malformed TopicMessage that breaks the sequenceNumber chain and prevents subsequent legitimate transactions from being gossiped to subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user cause gossip suppression by submitting a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a very large message payload (up to 6KiB) at high frequency, exhausting database write capacity and causing the importer to fall behind, after which the retention prune() deletes messages before they are gossiped to any subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that prune() is annotated @Modifying without explicit flush/clear semantics to cause stale first-level cache reads in the same transaction, where a subscriber retrieves a TopicMessage object that has already been pruned from the database, leading to phantom gossip of deleted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user prevent gossip of a transaction by exploiting the TopicMessage compareTo() method's COMPARATOR (nullsFirst on topicId, then sequenceNumber) to inject a null-topicId message that sorts before all legitimate messages, disrupting the ordering invariant relied upon by the subscriber's onNext() check and causing an IllegalStateException that terminates the subscription stream?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gap between when prune() deletes a TopicMessage and when the safety check in TopicMessageServiceImpl fires (after 1 second delay), such that the safety check's gap recovery query returns zero results for the pruned range, causing the subscriber to permanently miss those transactions without any error signal?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMessage sequenceNumber field (long, not validated for monotonicity at the repository layer) to submit a message with sequenceNumber = Long.MAX_VALUE, causing integer overflow in the missingMessages() calculation (current.getSequenceNumber() - last.getSequenceNumber() - 1) and triggering an incorrect gap recovery that skips legitimate transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user prevent gossip of a transaction by exploiting the TopicMessageFilter's startTime validation (startTime <= DomainUtils.now()) to craft a subscription request with startTime just before a prune() execution, such that the historical retrieval window is pruned away before the query executes, returning an empty result and causing the subscriber to miss all transactions in that window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMessage entity's @Id consensusTimestamp field (a primitive long) to cause a collision with an existing record by submitting a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction whose network-assigned consensusTimestamp matches a previously pruned slot, causing the importer to attempt an insert that violates no constraint (since the old record was deleted) but overwrites the expected sequenceNumber, corrupting the gossip chain?
